title: Efficient testing of carrier frequency in a population
abstract: Methods of detecting an infectious agent in a subject of a population, comprising: receiving for each subject of the population a biological sample; generating a number of pools of sample wherein the number of pools and the number of samples per pool is determined based on the total number of samples and the expected rate of infection carrier frequency in the population; detecting the presence or absence of the molecule of interest in each pool; and determining individual samples comprising the molecule of interest by applying an optimization algorithm, are provided. Computer program products to perform the same are also provided.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method of detecting an infectious agent in a population, the method comprising: a. receiving for each subject of said population a biological sample, wherein said biological sample from a subject infected with said infectious agent comprises a molecule of interest detectable by a diagnostic device; b. generating a number of pools of samples, wherein each pool comprises a number of samples greater than 1 and wherein each sample is within at least two pools and wherein said number of pools and said number of samples per pool is determined based on the total number of samples, and an expected rate of infection carrier frequency within said population; c. detecting, using said diagnostic device, a presence or absence of said molecule of interest within each of said pools to generate pool-specific infection data; and d. determining individual samples comprising said molecule of interest by applying an optimization algorithm to said pool-specific infection data; thereby detecting an infectious agent in a subject of a population. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein said sample is a biological fluid or wherein said receiving comprises receiving a solution generated by swabbing an area of a subject; optionally wherein said area is selected from the nose, mouth and throat. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein said sample comprises cells, and further comprising lysing said cells before performing said detecting. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein said lysing occurs after said generating pools of samples. 5. The method of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein said molecule of interest is selected from an infectious agent DNA, RNA, and protein and wherein said molecule of interest is not found in healthy subjects. 6. The method of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein said number of pools and said number of samples per pool is determined by a pooling calculation which divides said samples into said number of pools with said number of samples per pool based on said number of samples and said expected rate of infection carrier frequency. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein said pooling calculation is further based on a sensitivity of the diagnostic device to said molecule of interest. 8. The method of claim 6 or 7, wherein said pooling calculation comprises an error correction code. 9. The method of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein an expected rate of infection of about 1% indicates a number of pools that is about l/8th of the total number of samples and wherein each pool comprises about l/8th of the samples. 10. The method of claim 1 to 9, wherein a higher expected rate of infection requires a greater number of pools, fewer samples per pool or both. 11. The method of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein said detecting a presence or absence of said molecule of interest comprises a binary output of said diagnostic device indicating said presence or absence of said molecule of interest. 12. The method of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein said diagnostic device outputs a measurement of a level of said molecule of interest and said detecting a presence of said molecule of interest comprises detecting a level of said molecule of interest beyond a predetermined threshold. 13. The method of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein said molecule of interest is a nucleic acid molecule, said diagnostic device is a PCR machine or a nucleic acid sequencer, said detecting comprises performing PCR or next generation sequencing (NGS) on each of said pools and wherein the presence of an amplification product in a pool indicates the presence of said molecule of interest. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein said PCR comprises primers specific for said nucleic acid molecule. 15. The method of any one of claims 12 to 14, wherein said predetermined threshold is a Ct value of below 40 and wherein a Ct value below 40 indicates the presence of said molecule of interest. 16. The method of any one of claims 1 to 15, wherein said optimization algorithm is a gradient projection for sparse reconstruction (GPSR) algorithm. 17. The method of any one of claims 1 to 16, further comprising if a determined rate of infection with said population is at least 1% greater than said expected rate of infection, reperforming steps (b-d) wherein said generating pools is based on said determined rate of infection and not said expected rate of infection. 18. The method of any one of claims 1 to 17, further comprising if a determined rate of infection within said population is at least 0.5% greater than said expected rate of infection, directly determining the presence or absence of a molecule of interest indicative of said infection in samples for which the analysis was inconclusive due to the incorrect expected rate of infection. 19. The method of any one of claims 1 to 18, wherein said method is a computerized method. 20. The method of any one of claims 1 to 19, wherein said method is a method of detecting a plurality of infectious agents in said population, wherein said biological sample from a subject infected with an infectious agent of said plurality comprises a molecule of interest detectable by said diagnostic device and indicative of said infectious agent of said plurality, and wherein said detecting comprises simultaneously detecting molecules of interest indicative of each infectious agent of said plurality. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein said number of pools of samples, the number of samples per pool or both are unchanged due to detecting a plurality of infectious agents. 22. The method of claim 20 or 21, wherein each molecule of interest indicative of a different infectious agent of said plurality is uniquely detectable. 23. The method of any one of claims 20 to 22, wherein said expected rate of infection carrier frequency is the highest expected rate of infection carrier frequency of any of said infectious agents of said plurality. 24. The method of any one of claims 20 to 23, wherein the plurality of infectious agents is a plurality of genomic variants of a single infectious agent. 25. The method of any one of claims 1 to 24, further comprising administering a therapeutic agent capable of treating said infectious agent to a subject determined to be infected by said infectious agent. 26. A computer program product comprising a non-transitory computer-readable storage medium having program instructions embodied therewith, the program instructions executable by at least one hardware processor to: a. receive for each subject of a population a biological sample, wherein said biological sample from a subject infected with an infectious agent comprises a molecule of interest detectable by a diagnostic device; b. generate a number of pools of samples, wherein each pool comprises a number of samples greater than 1 and wherein each sample is within at least two pools and wherein said number of pools and said number of samples per pool is determined based on the total number of samples, and the expected rate of infection carrier frequency within said population; c. detect, using said diagnostic device, a presence or absence of said molecule of interest within each of said pools to generate pool-specific infection data; d. determine individual samples comprising said molecule of interest by applying an optimization algorithm to said pool-specific infection data; and e. output the samples that are infected by said infectious agent.
